specificity.description: Steps reference exact API paths, field names, status codes

verifiability.description: Expected results are measurable and unambiguous

completeness.description: Covers the full workflow including setup and cleanup

accuracy.description: Matches actual codebase behavior and constraints

rewrites.reason: Vague expected_result said 'handles consistently' without specifying exact behavior. Codebase uses Date.now() > LEGACY_TOKEN_CUTOFF where LEGACY_TOKEN_CUTOFF = new Date('2026-03-18T00:00:00Z').getTime(), so exact cutoff is exclusive (> means 2026-03-18T00:00:00Z is accepted). Rewritten with exact boundary logic.
Missing detail that verifyUserIdWithMeta only handles parts.length===2 or parts.length===3, returning null for other lengths. Added specific code path.
Missing detail on the exact check: parseInt('abc',10) returns NaN, Number.isFinite(NaN) is false, so returns null. Rewritten with exact validation path.

Token expiration check is now - issuedAt > TOKEN_MAX_AGE_SECONDS. A future issuedAt makes now - issuedAt negative, which is < 604800, so it actually passes the age check. The real rejection is issuedAt <= 0 check since future timestamps are > 0. Rewritten for accuracy.

Vague 'handled consistently' expected result. Code uses `now - issuedAt > TOKEN_MAX_AGE_SECONDS` (strict greater than), so exactly 604800 is NOT greater than 604800, meaning the token is accepted. Rewritten with exact boundary.
Added detail that parts.length would be 1, which matches neither the 2-part nor 3-part branch, so verifyUserIdWithMeta returns null at line 87.

Original said 'canViewServices permission' but the actual code uses hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewServiceCalls'). Also lacked specifics on SLA auto-calculation flow (prisma.slaPolicy.findUnique with companyId_priority compound key). Rewritten with exact field names, function calls, and Inngest event details.

Good structure but needed explicit mention of the tx.$queryRawUnsafe SELECT FOR UPDATE SQL, the Inngest event name 'ticket/activity-log', and the serviceMutation rate limit prefix 'svc-mut'. Rewritten for precision.

Original referenced 'canViewServices' but actual permission is 'canViewServiceCalls'. Also lacked the Inngest 'ticket/notification' event type='comment' detail. Added rate limit reference and isInternal parameter.

Original was vague about automation triggering. Rewritten to include Inngest event array construction with both 'ticket/activity-log' and 'ticket/status-change' events, and the direct fallback path for automation execution.
Added explicit validateUserInCompany call, Inngest notification event with type='assignee' and isNew=false, and the condition assigneeId !== currentUser.id for notification.
Added reference to getClosedTickets query (where status='CLOSED') and getTickets filtering (status IN ['OPEN','IN_PROGRESS','WAITING','RESOLVED']). Made verification steps more measurable.
Added exact details: validRestoreStatuses Set, SELECT FOR UPDATE SQL, slaResponseDueDate only recalculated when newStatus='OPEN', TicketActivityLog fieldName='status' with Hebrew labels.

Original said 'atomic deleteMany removes ticket and all associated records in a single transaction' but permanentlyDeleteTicket uses prisma.ticket.deleteMany (not a transaction). Cascade is handled by PostgreSQL FK constraints. Added Redis cache invalidation detail.
Original was too vague about how Kanban drag-and-drop connects to server logic. Added that it invokes the same updateTicket server action with row-level locking, not a separate endpoint.
Original said 'required fields (title, priority)' but createTicket also requires status and type. Added note about no Inngest notification when assigneeId is null.

Original described 'OPEN to CLOSED' as invalid transition, but the codebase does NOT enforce a state machine for ticket transitions (only enum validation). Rewritten to test enum validation instead (invalid string not in TicketStatus).
Same issue as TC-102: the code validates enum values, not transition graphs. Rewritten to test invalid priority enum value instead.
Added the exact Hebrew error message 'רק מי ששלח את ההודעה או מנהל יכול לערוך' and the admin bypass condition (user.role !== 'admin').
Added the exact Hebrew error message 'רק מי ששלח את ההודעה או מנהל יכול למחוק' and the admin bypass condition.
Original said 'canViewServices' but the actual permission flag is 'canViewServiceCalls'. Added explicit reference to the WHERE clause and companyId scoping.
Corrected from 'canViewServices' to 'canViewServiceCalls'. Added reference to assertServiceAccess and hasUserFlag function.
Original said '30 per user per minute' which is correct (RATE_LIMITS.serviceMutation.max=30) but lacked Redis key format and prefix. Added 'svc-mut' prefix and checkActionRateLimit details.
Added 'svc-read' prefix and Redis key format for rate limiting.
Added Hebrew label mapping details (statusMap in updateTicket fallback) and Inngest event structure.
Original was vague about how SLA breach is detected. Added specifics about SlaBreach model fields (breachType, status=PENDING) and getTicketStats counting PENDING breaches.
Original said 'empty items array' is OK, but createQuoteSchema requires min(1, 'At least one item is required'). Rewritten to test this as an error case instead.
Added exact details: requireProductUser function, createProductSchema.parse, Serializable transaction with count+create, price Number conversion, and revalidatePath('/services').
Added explicit reference to getProductsForDropdown query filter (where: { isActive: true }) and how the deactivated product is excluded.
Added exact transaction logic: findFirst with max quoteNumber, nextQuoteNumber calculation, crypto.randomUUID() for shareToken, Inngest event dedup ID format.
Original said 'Send the quote' generically. Rewritten with updateQuote details: pdfUrl reset to null, items delete-all-recreate strategy, Inngest with oldPdfUrl.
Added total calculation logic (reduce), getQuoteById product join details, and QuoteItem FK relationship.
Added explicit note that getProductsForDropdown only calls getCurrentUser(), does NOT call hasUserFlag. This is the key differentiator.
CRITICAL FIX: Original test said 'create quote with no line items succeeds' but createQuoteSchema has items.min(1). Rewritten as error case testing the min(1) validation.
Added exact sanitizeError mapping and the full error message: 'Cannot delete product: it is referenced by quote items. Deactivate it instead.'
Added note that the Prisma schema does NOT have a unique constraint on [companyId, sku] - only @@index([companyId, name]). The P2002 test may not be applicable unless there's a DB-level constraint.
CRITICAL FIX: Original said 'DRAFT to ACCEPTED is invalid transition' but there's NO state machine in the codebase. Only Zod enum validation. Rewritten to test invalid enum value instead.
CRITICAL FIX: Same issue - no state machine. The codebase allows any valid QuoteStatus on update. Rewritten to document this as expected behavior (no transition enforcement).
Corrected from 'canViewServices' to the exact code path: requireProductUser which calls hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewServices') and throws 'Forbidden'.
Added tokensMatch function, CUID_RE regex validation, 404 response on invalid token (anti-enumeration), and publicDownload rate limit details.
CRITICAL FIX: Original said 'REJECTED to SENT is rejected' but there's no state machine. Rewritten to test canViewQuotes permission check instead.

Original lacked exact server action name, field validations, nextDueDate calculation, and paymentMode behavior. Rewritten with specific createRetainer call, expected fields in response, and postpaid default nextDueDate logic.

Original was vague about which endpoint to call and what transaction behavior to expect. Rewritten with specific updateRetainer server action, atomic transaction with companyId guard, and whitelisted fields mention.

Original was unclear about whether cancel means status change or soft-delete. Rewritten to clarify that status='cancelled' is distinct from soft-delete (deletedAt), and that the record still appears in getRetainers() since it filters by deletedAt=null not status.
Original lacked specific API call and expected response structure. Rewritten with getFinanceRecords() call, stats object structure {income, expenses, profit}, and groupBy aggregation details.
Original was good but lacked the specific OR conditions for date range fallback (paidDate null + updatedAt). Rewritten with exact parallel aggregate queries and OR condition details.
Added specific prisma.retainer.count call and where clause details including companyId and status='active'.
Added specific code path (filters.source='FINANCE_RECORD' branch), the hardcoded type='INCOME' assignment, and the startOfDay/endOfDay normalization.
Original expected 2 matches but should match 2 (Client Meeting by title, Planning by description). Added safeQuery truncation to 200 chars detail and mode:'insensitive' specifics.

Original had incorrect formula using referenceBase for targetValue=0 case. Actually with targetValue=0 and currentValue=10 > 0, it enters the else branch using Math.round((targetValue/currentValue)*100). Corrected the expected result.
Added code-level detail: const startDate = startOfDay(startDateRaw) and const endDate = endOfDay(endDateRaw) using date-fns functions.
Original didn't mention the cascade soft-delete of associated retainer payments. Rewritten to include tx.oneTimePayment.updateMany with notes containing Hebrew retainer reference.
Added specific deleteFinanceRecord behavior (sets deletedAt=new Date()), the where clause filter (deletedAt: null), and that stats are also computed with the same filter.
Added exact JSON body example, Serializable isolation level reference, cache invalidation step, and all selected fields in the response.
Original lacked detail on the two-step archive process (compute + transaction). Rewritten with prisma.$transaction wrapping fresh goal read and _archivedSnapshot structure.

Original had unclear expected status determination. Rewritten with exact math: progressPercent=60, 60 < warningThreshold=70 so status=WARNING (not ON_TRACK as originally implied), plus dailyRate and projectedValue calculation.
Added exact status logic code path showing the if/else chain: EXCEEDED -> ON_TRACK -> WARNING -> CRITICAL.
Added detail about Error('MAX_GOALS') being thrown inside the transaction and caught by the outer handler which checks msg === 'MAX_GOALS'.
Original didn't clearly distinguish between API route (z.number().positive()) and server action (z.number().min(0)). Rewritten to highlight this inconsistency.
Added detail that GET route checks canViewFinance FIRST then canViewGoals, and the exact permission check functions used.
Original lacked exact schema fields (slug, createdBy, deletedAt). Rewritten with specific model fields from prisma schema.
Added specific prisma.record.create call, data JSON column structure, and retrieval verification.
Original was vague about cleanup. Rewritten with exact deleteRecordWithCleanup steps: file unlinking, attachment deletion, audit log unlinking, finance cascade, and new audit log creation.
Added take+1 pattern for hasMore detection, skip=1 for cursor pagination, and explicit hasMore/nextCursor expectations.
Original was vague about import mechanics. Rewritten with processImportFile parameters, csv-parse options (bom, delimiter, columns), and summary structure.
Original title said 'missing headers' but actual validation is per-field type. Rewritten to focus on date format validation with exact regex and Hebrew error messages.
Original was vague about bulk delete flow. Rewritten with exact API endpoint, validation steps (array check, integer filter, 5000 cap, same-table check, permission check), and Inngest event dispatch.

Original lacked specific API path (server action name), exact field defaults (minAdvanceHours=24, maxAdvanceDays=30), shareToken mention, and return format { success: true, data }. Rewritten with exact createMeetingType call, all input fields, and expected response structure.

Original was vague about the booking flow. Rewritten to reference exact POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book endpoint, request body fields, transaction atomicity (prisma.$transaction), and specific entities created (Meeting, CalendarEvent, Client).
Original lacked server action name and notification verification. Added updateMeetingStatus call, Hebrew notification label 'מאושר', and exact response format.
Original missed cancelMeeting specific behavior: cancelledAt/cancelledBy/cancelReason fields, fireMeetingAutomations call, and MAX_CANCEL_REASON_LENGTH truncation.
Original was acceptable but lacked exact function params and SlotsByDate return type. Enhanced with specific getAvailableSlots params and result format.
Original had incorrect date reference (2026-03-15 as Sunday). Rewritten to be date-agnostic and reference actual AvailabilityBlock model fields (startDate, endDate, allDay).
Original missed the occupiedStart/occupiedEnd calculation detail and hasOverlap mechanism. Rewritten with exact buffer math and overlap detection logic.
Original described booking attempts but the actual dailyLimit check is in getAvailableSlots via meetingsPerDay param. Rewritten to match actual codebase behavior.
Original was vague. Rewritten with exact POST endpoint, $transaction atomicity, TOCTOU protection via isSlotAvailable inside transaction, and response format.

Original claimed PENDING->COMPLETED transition is rejected, but the actual code does NOT enforce state machine transitions (updateMeetingStatus only validates the status string is in validStatuses). Rewritten to document actual behavior accurately.

Original claimed COMPLETED->CONFIRMED is rejected as backward transition, but actual code has no transition validation. Rewritten to test invalid status string rejection instead, which is what the code actually does.

Original referenced wrong function name and fields (type='EMAIL' doesn't exist). Rewritten with exact createGlobalMeetingAutomation call, VALID_TRIGGER_TYPES/VALID_ACTION_TYPES, tier check, and actual response format.

Original lacked specifics about the OR query for rule matching and Hebrew date formatting. Rewritten with exact fireMeetingAutomations signature, rule matching query, and RULE_CONCURRENCY=5 batch processing.
Original was vague about deduplication mechanism. Rewritten with exact processMeetingReminders flow: dedupSet construction, calendarEventId-based dedup, AutomationLog.createMany with skipDuplicates.
Original said 20/min limit but actual RATE_LIMITS.automationMutate is 30/min. Corrected to match codebase: { prefix: 'auto-mut', max: 30, windowSeconds: 60 }.
Enhanced with exact nameSchema validation details, the skip of depth check for null parentId, and exact prisma.folder.create call signature.
Enhanced with exact saveFileMetadataSchema validation fields and constraints, duplicate detection by key, and response format including downloadUrl pattern.
Enhanced with isSafeStorageUrl check, exact response header values, fetch timeout of 15000ms, and redirect:'error' option.
Original used wrong function name and non-existent type='EMAIL'. Corrected to createGlobalMeetingAutomation with actual VALID_TRIGGER_TYPES and VALID_ACTION_TYPES from codebase.
Original lacked OR query details and Hebrew date formatting. Added exact fireMeetingAutomations query structure and meetingContext with formatDateHe/formatTimeHe.
Original missed details about calendarEventId-based deduplication, dedupSet construction, and createMany with skipDuplicates:true.
Original stated 20/min but actual limit is 30/min (RATE_LIMITS.automationMutate.max=30). Corrected to match codebase.
Enhanced with nameSchema validation details, parentId=null behavior (skip depth check), and exact prisma.folder.create call.
Enhanced with full saveFileMetadataSchema field constraints, duplicate detection mechanism, and response format.
Enhanced with isSafeStorageUrl check, exact header values, fetch timeout, and Content-Length > 50MB guard.
